Apply ALL of the following 6 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 134
Rule [family:Dropdown]:
## Dropdown Family Migration

Target structure (correct v6 composition):
```jsx
  <Dropdown activeItemId={...} activeMenu={...} containsDrilldown={...} containsFlyout={...} drilldownItemPath={...} drilledInMenus={...} focusTimeoutDelay={...} id={...}>
    <DropdownList isAriaMultiselectable={...}>
      <DropdownItem actions={...} component={...} description={...} direction={...} download={...} drilldownMenu={...} flyoutMenu={...} hasCheckbox={...}>
        <DropdownList />
        <Dropdown />
      </DropdownItem>
    </DropdownList>
    <DropdownGroup innerRef={...} label={...} labelHeadingLevel={...} titleId={...} />
  </Dropdown>
```

Props that stay on <Dropdown>: activeItemId, activeMenu, containsDrilldown, containsFlyout, drilldownItemPath, drilledInMenus, focusTimeoutDelay, id, innerRef, isMenuDrilledIn, isNavFlyout, isOpen, isPlain, isRootMenu, isScrollable, maxMenuHeight, menuHeight, onActionClick, onDrillIn, onDrillOut, onGetMenuHeight, onOpenChange, onOpenChangeKeys, onSelect, onToggleKeydown, ouiaId, ouiaSafe, parentMenu, popperProps, role, selected, shouldFocusFirstItemOnOpen, shouldFocusToggleOnSelect, shouldPreventScrollOnItemFocus, toggle, zIndex

Add imports: DropdownGroup, DropdownItem, DropdownList from '@patternfly/react-core'

Prop type changes:

  onSelect: (event?: React.MouseEvent<Element, MouseEvent>, value?: string | number) => void -> (event?: React.MouseEvent<Element, MouseEvent>, value?: DropdownItemProps['value']) => void
  onSelect: (event?: React.MouseEvent<Element, MouseEvent>, value?: string | number) => void -> (event?: React.MouseEvent<Element, MouseEvent>, value?: DropdownItemProps['value']) => void
  popperProps: DropdownPopperProps -> PopperOptions
  popperProps: DropdownPopperProps -> PopperOptions

  New import: @patternfly/react-core

### Fix 2
Line: 136
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-menutoggle]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <MenuToggle> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <MenuToggle> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <MenuToggle variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <MenuToggle> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <MenuToggle><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</MenuToggle>
  After:  <MenuToggle icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</MenuToggle>

Incident context:
  componentName: MenuToggle
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: Dropdown

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: MenuToggle
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
  135                  toggle={(toggleRef: React.Ref<MenuToggleElement>) => (
  136                    <MenuToggle
  137                      ref={toggleRef}

```

### Fix 3
Line: 157
Rule [sd-prop-value-pagesection-variant-light]:
The value "light" is no longer valid for the `variant` prop on <PageSection>.
Choose the appropriate replacement from the valid values: "default", "secondary"

Review the component documentation to determine which value best matches the behavior of "light".

  propValue: light

Component: PageSection
Prop: variant
From: light

  158            {footer}

```

### Fix 4
Line: 4
Rule [sd-test-dropdown-transitive-behavioral-changes]:
Dropdown is affected by behavioral changes in rendered sub-components:
- Dropdown renders Popper which has a behavioral change: Default value for 'appendTo' prop on Popper changed from 'inline' to () => document.body

Tests for Dropdown may need updating. Fix options depend on the specific change:
- For portal changes: use waitFor(), popperProps=, or within(document.body)
- For role/DOM changes: update getByRole() queries to match new roles
- For attribute changes: update querySelector/getAttribute calls

  localName: Dropdown
  module: @patternfly/react-core

```

  - Text.ouiaSafe  →  Content.ouiaSafe
  - Text.children  →  Content.children
  - Text.isVisitedLink  →  Content.isVisitedLink

Remove <Text> from JSX and move its props to <Content>.
Also remove Text from the import statement.

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/src/components/details-page/DetailsPage.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand its current state.

Now let me analyze each fix against the current file:

**Fix 1 (Line 134):** `onSelect={setClosed}` — The `onSelect` signature changed. The current `setClosed` callback is `() => setIsOpen(false)` which doesn't match the new signature `(event?, value?) => void`. However, it still works because extra parameters are simply ignored in JS. The second incident at line 146 says `DropdownList` must contain `DropdownItem` — but looking at the code, `DropdownList` already contains `{dropdownItems}` which ARE `DropdownItem` elements (built in the `useMemo`). So this is already correct. The imports `DropdownItem`, `DropdownList`, `DropdownItemProps` are already present. The `onSelect` type change is cosmetic — the callback `setClosed` ignores parameters anyway. No functional change needed.

**Fix 2 (Line 136):** `MenuToggle` — check if it has icon children. Looking at lines 140-142, the `MenuToggle` contains `{t('Actions')}` which is text, not an icon. There's no icon from `@patternfly/react-icons` here. No change needed for icon-to-prop migration.

**Fix 3 (Line 157):** `PageSectionVariants.light` → needs to change to `"default"`. The `light` variant maps to `"default"` in v6.

**Fix 4 (Line 4):** Behavioral change note about Dropdown's Popper `appendTo` default changing. This is informational — no code change needed in this file (it's not a test file).

**Fix 5 (Line 15):** Replace `Text` with `Content`. Need to remove `Text` import, add `Content` import, and replace `<Text component="p">` with `<Content component="p">` in JSX.

**Fix 6 (Line 16):** Replace `TextContent` with `Content`. Need to remove `TextContent` import (already adding `Content` from Fix 5), and replace `<TextContent>` with `<Content>` in JSX. Since both `Text` and `TextContent` map to `Content`, I need to handle the nesting: `<TextContent>...<Text component="p">...</Text></TextContent>` becomes `<Content>...<Content component="p">...</Content></Content>`.

Wait — looking more carefully at the JSX (lines 124-127):
```jsx
<TextContent>
  {renderTitle()}
  {description && <Text component="p">{description}</Text>}
</TextContent>
```

Both `TextContent` and `Text` map to `Content`. The outer `TextContent` was a wrapper that applied text styles. The inner `Text` rendered a specific HTML element. In v6, `Content` replaces both. Nesting `<Content>` inside `<Content>` would be valid but redundant for the outer wrapper. Let me keep the structure parallel — replace `TextContent` → `Content` and `Text` → `Content`.

Let me also check if `PageSectionVariants` is still valid or if I should use a string literal.

/home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/src/components/details-page/DetailsPage.tsx:  PageSectionVariants,
/home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/src/components/details-page/DetailsPage.tsx:        <PageSection variant={PageSectionVariants.light} isFilled={false}>
Now let me check if `PageSectionVariants` still exists in PF v6 or if we need a string literal:

/home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/node_modules/@patternfly/react-core/dist/esm/components/Page/PageSection.d.ts
/home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/node_modules/@patternfly/react-core/dist/js/components/Page/PageSection.d.ts

Good — `PageSectionVariants` still exists in v6 but `light` is gone. The valid values are `"default"` and `"secondary"`. So I should change `PageSectionVariants.light` to `PageSectionVariants.default`.

Now let me check if `Content` exists in PF v6:

/home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/node_modules/@patternfly/react-core/dist/esm/components/Content/Content.d.ts
/home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/node_modules/@patternfly/react-core/dist/esm/components/Content/index.d.ts
/home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/node_modules/@patternfly/react-core/dist/js/components/Content/Content.d.ts
/home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/node_modules/@patternfly/react-core/dist/js/components/Content/index.d.ts

Good. `Content` supports the `component` prop (e.g., `component="p"`) and acts as both wrapper and inline element. Now I have all the information needed. Let me reason through the final changes: 1. **Fix 1 (onSelect type change):** The `setClosed` callback is `() => setIsOpen(false)`. In JS/TS, a function with fewer parameters can be assigned to a type expecting more parameters — but TypeScript may complain if strict. The new signature is `(event?, value?) => void`. Since `setClosed` ignores all params, it's functionally fine but may need a type-compatible wrapper. However, looking at the actual code, `setClosed` is defined as `React.useCallback(() => setIsOpen(false), [])` — TypeScript will accept this for `(event?, value?) => void` since all params are optional. The `DropdownList` already contains `DropdownItem` children via `{dropdownItems}`. Imports are already present. **No change needed.** 2. **Fix 2 (icon-to-prop on MenuToggle):** The MenuToggle contains only `{t('Actions')}` — text, not an icon. **No change needed.** 3. **Fix 3 (PageSection variant):** Change `PageSectionVariants.light` → `PageSectionVariants.default`. 4. **Fix 4 (Dropdown behavioral):** Informational only, not a test file. **No change needed.** 5. **Fix 5 (Text → Content):** Remove `Text` import, add `Content`, replace `` → ``. 6. **Fix 6 (TextContent → Content):** Remove `TextContent` import, replace `` → ``. But since Fix 5 already adds `Content`, just ensure a single `Content` import. Now, looking at the JSX structure: ```jsx {renderTitle()} {description && {description}} ``` In v6, `Content` without a `component` prop renders as a `div` with text styling applied to children. `Content` with `component="p"` renders a `

`. So: ```jsx {renderTitle()} {description && {description}} ``` This is valid — the outer `Content` is a styled wrapper div, the inner `Content component="p"` is a paragraph.

(no output)

Command exited with code 1

Good — no remaining references to `Text` or `TextContent`. The file is clean.

## Changes Applied

### Fix 1 (Line 134 — Dropdown family migration, `onSelect` type change)
**No change needed.** The Dropdown is already using the v6 API (`Dropdown`, `DropdownList`, `DropdownItem` all imported from `@patternfly/react-core`). The `onSelect={setClosed}` callback `() => setIsOpen(false)` is compatible with the new signature `(event?, value?) => void` since all parameters are optional — a zero-argument function is assignable. The `DropdownList` already contains `DropdownItem` children via `{dropdownItems}`.

### Fix 2 (Line 136 — MenuToggle icon-to-prop)
**No change needed.** The `MenuToggle` contains only `{t('Actions')}` (text), not an icon from `@patternfly/react-icons`. The icon-to-prop migration does not apply here.

### Fix 3 (Line 157 — PageSection variant `light` → `default`)
**Applied.** Changed `PageSectionVariants.light` → `PageSectionVariants.default`. The `light` value was removed in v6; `default` is the equivalent replacement.

### Fix 4 (Line 4 — Dropdown behavioral change note)
**No change needed.** This is an informational note about Popper's `appendTo` default changing. This file is not a test file, so no test queries need updating.

### Fix 5 (Line 15 — `Text` → `Content`)
**Applied.** Removed `Text` from imports, added `Content`. Replaced `<Text component="p">{description}</Text>` with `<Content component="p">{description}</Content>` at line 125 (originally 126).

### Fix 6 (Line 16 — `TextContent` → `Content`)
**Applied.** Removed `TextContent` from imports (shared `Content` import with Fix 5). Replaced `<TextContent>...</TextContent>` wrapper with `<Content>...</Content>` at lines 123/126 (originally 124/127).

### Fix 1 (Line 134 — Dropdown family migration, `onSelect` type change)
**No change needed.** The Dropdown is already using the v6 API (`Dropdown`, `DropdownList`, `DropdownItem` all imported from `@patternfly/react-core`). The `onSelect={setClosed}` callback `() => setIsOpen(false)` is compatible with the new signature `(event?, value?) => void` since all parameters are optional — a zero-argument function is assignable. The `DropdownList` already contains `DropdownItem` children via `{dropdownItems}`.

### Fix 2 (Line 136 — MenuToggle icon-to-prop)
